codeReview.summary: Solid implementation of GET /api/health-data and GET /api/health-data/types endpoints. Code is well-structured, follows existing codebase patterns (encryption, auth, error envelope, fire-and-forget audit events), and has comprehensive test coverage (24 tests). The main issues are minor: response schema for the /types endpoint deviates from the LLD field names (uses date_range.start/end and count instead of earliest_date/latest_date/data_point_count), two LLD-specified validations are missing (start-not-in-future and max-5-year date range span), and significant code duplication exists with the viewer/data route. All core requirements from the feature description are met: decryption, daily/weekly/monthly resolution, viewer scope enforcement, empty-array-not-404, audit events, and auth enforcement.

issues.description: Types endpoint response field names deviate from LLD Section 7.3.2. LLD specifies 'earliest_date', 'latest_date', 'data_point_count' but implementation uses 'date_range: { start, end }' and 'count'. The implementation matches the feature description text, but not the LLD spec. If frontend consumers are built from the LLD, this will cause a mismatch.

Category values use PascalCase from the metric registry ('Sleep', 'Cardio', 'Activity') but the LLD Section 7.3.2 shows lowercase ('sleep', 'cardiovascular', 'activity'). Minor inconsistency that could affect frontend filtering.

Missing two LLD-specified validation rules: (1) 'start: Valid date, not in the future' and (2) 'Date range: Maximum span of 1,825 days (5 years)'. The feature description didn't explicitly require these, but the LLD validation rules section does.

Significant code duplication with src/app/api/viewer/data/route.ts. The DataPoint interface, getIsoWeekMonday(), getFirstOfMonth(), aggregatePoints() functions, and much of the query/decrypt/response logic are copy-pasted. These should be extracted into a shared utility (e.g., src/lib/health-data/query.ts) for maintainability.

In aggregatePoints(), the 'source' for aggregated periods is taken from the first point encountered. If a user has data from multiple sources for the same metric in the same week/month, the aggregated source will be arbitrary. Consider returning 'mixed' or an array of sources for multi-source periods.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The /types endpoint response field naming deviates from LLD Section 7.3.2 (date_range.start/end vs earliest_date/latest_date, count vs data_point_count). The feature description used different naming than the LLD. AGENTS.md should clarify that the LLD is the canonical API contract and feature descriptions should not override field names.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/app/api/health-data/types/route.ts:62-70 returns { date_range: { start, end }, count } but LLD Section 7.3.2 response example shows { earliest_date, latest_date, data_point_count }

sharedStateObservations.observation: Health data query/decrypt/aggregate logic is duplicated across two routes (health-data/route.ts and viewer/data/route.ts). The DataPoint interface, aggregation helpers (getIsoWeekMonday, getFirstOfMonth, aggregatePoints), and core query+decrypt+response flow are nearly identical. This could be documented as a pattern to extract into a shared module.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: health-data/route.ts lines 55-100 (aggregation helpers) are identical to viewer/data/route.ts lines 55-100. Query/decrypt/response logic (lines 160-280) is also near-identical.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker reported followedProcedure: true, but the transcript shows tests and implementation were created sequentially without running tests in the 'red' phase first (the skill procedure step 3 says 'Run bun run test — tests should FAIL (red phase)'). The worker created tests, then implementation, then ran all tests together. This is a minor procedural deviation that doesn't affect quality.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton shows: Create test file -> Create route.ts -> Create types/route.ts -> Execute bun run test (all pass). No intermediate test run to verify red phase.

summary: PASS. The api-health-data feature is well-implemented with comprehensive test coverage (24 tests). Both endpoints (GET /api/health-data and GET /api/health-data/types) correctly handle auth enforcement, input validation, data decryption, resolution aggregation (daily/weekly/monthly), viewer scope enforcement (metric filtering + date clamping), empty result handling (200 not 404), and audit event emission. Non-blocking issues found: (1) types endpoint field names deviate from LLD spec (match feature description instead), (2) category casing mismatch vs LLD, (3) missing LLD-specified validations (start-not-in-future, max-5-year range), (4) significant code duplication with viewer/data route, (5) aggregation source handling for mixed-source periods. All tests, typecheck, and lint pass.
